mechanism_narrative: Rising U.S. industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This effect is persistent across the business cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum over 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the correlation is negative in more than 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, it reflects a tight labor market with strong demand for workers, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher S&P 500 earnings and prices over the next quarter. The signal activates only when UNRATE is in the sub-5% zone.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE is below 5.0%, the median forward 60-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) provide a consistent signal of broad-based labor market improvement. This dual confirmation reduces uncertainty about consumer demand and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is falling, the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not statistically greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of monetary tightening defined by a federal funds rate above 4% and CPI above 200 (index), high borrowing costs and inflation pressure compress equity valuations and reduce future earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. The interaction of both high rates and high inflation amplifies the negative effect.
regime_hint: tightening cycle when FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 200
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 200, the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of instances or the median return is not significantly below zero (Wilcoxon p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts above 1,500 thousand units signals robust residential construction activity, which stimulates related industries, raises consumer wealth, and boosts overall economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in 40% or more of cases, or the average return is not significantly higher than during periods below 1500 (two-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a synchronized expansion in both consumer demand and manufacturing output. This combination reduces inventory overhang and boosts corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO are rising month-over-month, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of such episodes, or the mean return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects improving labor demand and falling slack, which lifts disposable income and corporate earnings. This leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 1-week horizon, acting as a high-frequency signal of economic momentum.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following nonfarm payroll release days has a negative average over a 3-year rolling window, or the correlation between payroll growth and 5-day returns is insignificantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 210 (index level), it signals above-target inflation that prompts the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, compressing equity valuations and reducing risk appetite. This negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns materializes over a 20-day horizon as tightening expectations are repriced.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of instances, or the median return is not significantly negative (one-sample Wilcoxon test p > 0.10).
